headlines.title: Democratic socialist Kiros defeats longtime incumbent in Colorado primary
headlines.snippet: Melat Kiros, a 29-year-old former lawyer, defeated a longtime incumbent in Colorado's primary. Kiros has been critical of Democrats for their support of Israel during the conflict in Gaza.

headlines.title: ‘I did nothing wrong’: Sweden’s migration regime hardens, upending lives
headlines.snippet: Sweden has completed a decade-long overhaul of its migration regime, fundamentally changing its approach after previously being a major destination for refugees and migrants.

headlines.title: Why is MAGA in meltdown over the Supreme Court birthright ruling?
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has rejected Donald Trump's attempt to prevent children of certain immigrants from automatically obtaining US citizenship at birth.

headlines.title: Gojek co-founder, turned Indonesian Education Minister jailed for 10 years
headlines.snippet: Nadiem Makarim, who co-founded Gojek and previously served as Indonesian Education Minister, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison.

headlines.title: Tutoring centre roof collapses killing 14 children in Pakistan
headlines.snippet: The collapse of a tutoring center roof near Lahore, Pakistan, resulted in the deaths of at least 14 children.

headlines.title: Iran, Oman to jointly charge fees along Strait of Hormuz: Report
headlines.snippet: Iran, Oman to jointly charge fees along Strait of Hormuz: Report Iran and Oman are looking to jointly impose a service fee for use of the Strait of Hormuz, despite open US objections, The New York Times reported on Tuesday, citing an Iranian official and four diplomats.

headlines.title: Israeli settlers storm Al-Aqsa mosque
headlines.snippet: Israeli settlers storm Al-Aqsa mosque Israeli settlers have stormed the Al-Aqsa mosque courtyard in occupied East Jerusalem under the protection of Israeli police, the Palestinian news agency Wafa reports.

headlines.title: Thailand says 10 stranded vessels leave Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: Thailand's Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced that 10 Thai-flagged or chartered vessels have departed the Strait of Hormuz after being stranded there since late February. One vessel remains to load cargo and is expected to leave soon.

headlines.title: Modi urges diplomacy in call with Iran’s Pezeshkian
headlines.snippet: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, emphasizing the importance of freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz and advocating for dialogue to resolve disputes.

headlines.title: UN says one million remain displaced in Lebanon by Israel's war on country
headlines.snippet: The UN's humanitarian coordinator in Lebanon stated that approximately one million people remain displaced due to the ongoing war with Israel. The coordinator also noted that 1.4 million people in Lebanon require humanitarian assistance.
